description: Real-time event ingestion and routing pipeline. Connects event sources (Event Hubs, IoT Hub, custom endpoints) to destinations (KQL databases, lakehouses) with optional transformations. The streaming equivalent of a data pipeline.

definition_parts.description: Topology with sources, destinations, streams, operators, and compatibilityLevel

notes: Topology is graph-based: nodes reference each other via inputNodes arrays
Source types: CustomEndpoint, AzureEventHub, SampleData, AmazonKinesis, etc.
Destination types: Eventhouse, Lakehouse, CustomEndpoint, Activator
Eventhouse destination itemId must be KQL Database ID (not Eventhouse ID)
Use add-source/add-destination helpers to avoid manual definition crafting
CDC sources (AzureSQLDBCDC, AzureSQLMIDBCDC, SQLServerOnVMDBCDC, MySQLCDC, PostgreSQLCDC) support snapshotMode (Initial/InitialOnly/NoData) in --properties to customize the initial snapshot

SQL Server-family CDC sources only (AzureSQLDBCDC, AzureSQLMIDBCDC, SQLServerOnVMDBCDC) additionally support excludedColumns (comma-separated), databaseApplicationIntent (ReadWrite/ReadOnly), and snapshotSelectStatementOverrides ([{tableName, selectStatement}])

ApacheKafka and MQTT sources support tlsSettings.trustCACertificate / tlsSettings.clientCertificate, each referencing a KeyVault-backed certificate ({"type":"KeyVault","azureKeyVaultResourceId":"<ARM id>","certificateName":"<name>"})

FabricAnomalyDetectionEvents source properties: workspaceId, itemId, configurationId, includedEventTypes (e.g. Microsoft.Fabric.AnomalyDetection.AnomalyDetected), and optional Event Grid advanced filters
